reasoning:
The 30-day average of protests over the 360 days prior to October 2025 is ~3.167 (i.e. exactly 38 protests in that year). This average is heavily inflated by the intense "Vie Chère" demonstrations that occurred in late 2024 and early 2025. Comprehensive web searches covering the period of August through October 2025 yield no news evidence of major ongoing protests or strikes in Martinique. This indicates the territory is currently experiencing a quiet period, during which it is highly unlikely to register 4 or more protests per month (which is what would be needed to exceed the inflated 3.167 baseline). 

For the first resolution date (2025-11-02), 23 days of the 30-day window have already passed prior to the knowledge cutoff with no reports of protests, making a spike in recorded ACLED events very unlikely. Thus, the probability is low. The probability naturally increases for the subsequent resolution dates (2025-11-25 and 2026-01-24) as forecasting into the future allows more time for the potential rekindling of social unrest, particularly given Martinique's history of general strikes occurring in January and February. However, lacking any current catalyst in the news, probabilities for these dates still sit below 50%.
